title: Fluffy Polish Pork Casserole
description: A hearty Polish dish featuring tender pork, fluffy bread, and dried oregano, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe is onion and garlic free, making it suitable for those with sensitivities.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, cook the ground pork over medium heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, salt, and pepper.
Add the cooked pork, dried oregano, and shredded cheese to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Tear the white bread into small pieces and add it to the bowl, stirring until the bread is evenly distributed.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and smooth the top.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 35 minutes, or until it is puffed and golden brown.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
